profile.name: Abhijit Ojha
profile.headline: I turn complex systems into clear, durable engineering decisions.
profile.introduction: Staff Software Engineer with 13+ years of experience building enterprise software across Healthcare, Logistics, Retail, and Product Engineering.
profile.supportingCopy: I connect architecture, hands-on delivery, and calm technical leadership to help teams make consequential systems easier to evolve.
profile.availability: Open to Staff+ engineering leadership conversations

valuePillars.title: Boundaries that hold
valuePillars.copy: Clear seams, explicit failure modes, and a path from decision to delivery.

valuePillars.title: Trade-offs made visible
valuePillars.copy: Pragmatic choices balancing reliability, speed, operability, and change.

valuePillars.title: Teams that ship with confidence
valuePillars.copy: Hands-on guidance, quality habits, and shared context across disciplines.

records.title: Enterprise Archive
records.overview: Enterprise healthcare imaging archive work supporting mission-critical clinical imaging workflows.
records.challenge: Healthcare systems need reliable ingestion, storage, retrieval, and exchange of imaging studies across enterprise workflows.
records.solution: Designed and delivered archive and imaging capabilities spanning DICOM workflows, PACS integration, APIs, cloud services, and enterprise data storage.
records.architecture: The architecture includes DICOM producers and consumers, archive services, PACS integrations, REST APIs, messaging, databases, and AWS-hosted capabilities. The architecture is presented as a high-level flow.
responsibilities: Own architecture, design, implementation, delivery, and cross-team coordination.
Lead technical reviews and mentor engineers working on archive capabilities.
Improve backend performance, quality, maintainability, and test coverage.
impact: Strengthened engineering quality through automated testing and regression prevention.
decisions: Use service and API boundaries for independently evolving capabilities.
Use automated backend testing as a release-quality control.

records.title: PACS Prefetch
records.overview: Prefetch capability for making relevant PACS imaging studies available to downstream clinical workflows.
records.challenge: Clinical imaging consumers need prior or relevant studies available with dependable retrieval behavior and high availability.
records.solution: Owned the feature and applied PACS domain expertise to the retrieval workflow, performance, and operational behavior.
records.architecture: A retrieval workflow connects study selection and request handling with PACS/archive access and downstream availability.
impact: Improved the dependability of imaging-study availability for consuming workflows.

records.title: Archive Capacity Expansion
records.overview: Archive-scale enhancement designed to support a larger number of archive instances without destabilizing existing behavior.
records.challenge: The archive platform needed to scale its supported archive count while preserving compatibility and regression safety.
records.solution: Extended archive data store integrations, implemented custom scheduling behavior, and handled C++ conversions required by the feature.
records.architecture: The architecture combines archive configuration and scheduling logic with archive data store access and native-code conversion boundaries.
responsibilities: Design and implement the scale enhancement.
Protect existing behavior through regression-focused engineering.
impact: Expanded archive workflow capacity with dependable retrieval and maintainable operations.

records.title: Imaging Workflow Validation Tool
records.overview: An enterprise engineering engagement focused on dependable delivery.
records.challenge: Manual validation of image ingestion and imaging APIs is repetitive and time-consuming.
records.solution: Built an imaging validation harness-based WPF tool that exercises imaging flows and uses assisted validation services to assist validation work.
records.architecture: This architecture keeps implementation topology concise and focused.
responsibilities: Design and implement the WPF validation workflow.
Apply AI assistance to reduce repetitive engineering work.
impact: Streamlined imaging validation with repeatable checks and clearer quality signals.

records.title: Employee Feedback Tracking Tool
records.overview: An enterprise engineering engagement focused on dependable delivery.
records.challenge: Teams needed a lightweight way to capture and follow feedback information.
records.architecture: This work demonstrates an Express.js application with concise persistence, authentication, deployment, and UI boundaries.
responsibilities: Implement the tracking workflow and supporting application behavior.
impact: Provided a dedicated workflow for employee feedback tracking.

records.title: Service Boundary Modernization
records.overview: Modernization work involving migration of legacy capabilities toward microservices and improved engineering workflows.
records.challenge: Legacy application boundaries can constrain deployment, maintainability, and independent evolution.
records.solution: This work demonstrates microservices migration and developer-assistance tooling-assisted engineering, but keeps employer context general.
records.architecture: Target architecture uses decomposed services and independent delivery boundaries; the architecture is presented as a high-level flow.
responsibilities: Use developer-assistance tooling for documentation and SQL-related development tasks.
impact: Clarified service boundaries to make modernization easier to evolve and operate.

records.title: Enterprise Automation Framework
records.overview: Enterprise automation framework spanning Windows, web, SAP, and mainframe workflows.
records.challenge: Operational processes crossed several application types and required repeatable automation rather than isolated scripts.
records.solution: Built a shared RPA and .NET framework to automate workflows across heterogeneous enterprise systems.
records.architecture: A .NET/WPF control layer coordinates automation adapters for Windows, web, SAP, and mainframe systems, with SQL Server supporting application data. The architecture is presented as a high-level flow.
responsibilities: Develop framework capabilities and workflow integrations.
Support maintainable automation across multiple target system types.
impact: Created a common engineering foundation for cross-platform enterprise automation.

records.title: Booking Workflow Optimization
records.overview: Performance work for booking workflows.
records.challenge: Booking operations depended on data collection and processing paths that required performance improvement.
records.solution: Optimized the application and data-scraping flow to improve operational responsiveness.
records.architecture: This work demonstrates enterprise operations, booking data workflow data scraping, and performance optimization with concise component and data-flow details.
responsibilities: Analyze bottlenecks and implement performance improvements.
impact: Improved booking workflow reliability through focused performance and maintainability work.

records.title: Commodity Management Portal
records.overview: Portal supporting commodity operations workflows and counterparties.
records.challenge: Commodity-management users needed application support for trading-related workflows and financial calculations.
records.solution: Developed portal capabilities and unit tests for complex financial algorithms integrated with enterprise trading systems.
records.architecture: A C# application connects business workflows and tested calculation logic with Oracle and enterprise trading integrations.
responsibilities: Create unit tests for complex financial algorithms.
impact: Improved testability and delivery confidence for complex financial calculations.

records.title: Automated Demand Response
records.overview: Application supporting electricity-demand and future-bid workflows.
records.challenge: Energy stakeholders needed software to work with electricity-demand information and future bids.
records.solution: Delivered application features and data access across web and desktop UI technologies.
records.architecture: ASP.NET and WPF presentation layers use C# and Entity Framework to work with Oracle 11g data. The architecture is presented as a high-level flow.

records.title: Track Layout
records.overview: Windows application for validating conveyor motor configurations and generating configuration output files.
records.challenge: Industrial track layouts required validation of motor configurations and reliable generation of downstream configuration files.
records.solution: Built validation and generation workflows in a C# Windows Forms application.
records.architecture: A desktop application models layout inputs, applies motor/configuration validation, and emits configuration output files for downstream use.
impact: Automated configuration validation and file generation for track-layout workflows.

records.title: Capacity Planning Platform
records.overview: Capacity-planning platform covering locations, resources, seats, projects, and stakeholders.
records.challenge: Enterprise teams needed a unified system for planning physical and human capacity across organizational dimensions.
records.solution: Implemented web application and service capabilities for capacity-planning workflows.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET application uses C# business logic, WCF service boundaries, and SQL Server 2008 persistence.
impact: Centralized capacity-planning workflows across locations, resources, seats, projects, and stakeholders.

records.title: Procurement Approval Workflow
records.overview: Role-based procurement approval platform for enterprise operations.
records.challenge: Procurement approvals required controlled access and documented workflows across user roles.
records.solution: Contributed requirements and design documentation and developed a role-rights-based approval application.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET/C# application uses WCF services and SQL Server 2012, with jQuery and JavaScript in the web layer.
responsibilities: Contribute HLD, LLD, and SRS artifacts.

records.title: Legacy Logic Modernization
records.overview: Legacy modernization engagement for utility operations.
records.challenge: Existing legacy logic behavior needed to be understood and represented in database procedures while preserving completeness.
records.solution: Analyzed legacy logic, supported database procedures rewriting, documented behavior, and reviewed converted scope for completeness.
records.architecture: The modernization flow moves from legacy logic analysis to mapped database procedures behavior, supported by analysis and review artifacts.
responsibilities: Support database procedures rewrite and documentation.
Review converted functionality for completeness within a cross-functional team.
impact: Supported controlled modernization of legacy power-sector logic.

records.title: Public Asset Records Workflow
records.overview: Application for recording assets associated with government employees and political appointees for public-sector asset workflow.
records.challenge: The stakeholders needed structured collection and management of asset information for covered individuals.
records.solution: Contributed requirements, SRS, SOA design, WCF services, and application implementation.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET 3.5 application uses C#, WCF/SOA service boundaries, SQL Server 2008, jQuery, and JavaScript.
responsibilities: Contribute SOA/WCF design and implementation within a cross-functional team.
impact: Provided a structured digital workflow for public asset records.

records.title: Government Service Data Access
records.overview: public-sector services application engagement focused on persistence and production support.
records.challenge: The system required reliable data-access behavior and production support.
records.solution: Implemented DAL/persistence work and supported production issues.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET/VB.NET application uses a SQL Server 2008 data layer with jQuery and JavaScript in the UI.
responsibilities: Provide production support within a cross-functional team.
impact: Improved and supported production data-access workflows.

records.title: Utility Grievance and Reporting Platform
records.overview: Platform for enterprise operations supporting grievances, MIS, mobile, and payment services.
records.challenge: Power-distribution stakeholders needed digital grievance handling, reporting, mobile access, and payment integrations.
records.solution: Developed web application and web-service capabilities for the related workflows.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET/VB.NET application uses SQL Server 2005, jQuery, JavaScript, and service integrations for mobile and payment workflows.
responsibilities: Implement mobile and payment web-service integrations within a cross-functional team.
impact: Enabled digital grievance, reporting, mobile, and payment-related workflows for power-distribution organizations.

records.title: Education Monitoring and Reporting Platform
records.overview: Education monitoring platform for SSA/enterprise operations covering schools, teachers, children, and yearly reporting.
records.challenge: Education administrators needed consolidated monitoring and annual reporting across core school-system entities.
records.solution: Developed web workflows, reports, and ADO.NET-based data access.
records.architecture: An ASP.NET/VB.NET application uses SQL Server 2008 and ADO.NET DataAdapter, DataSet, and DataReader patterns, with jQuery in the UI.
responsibilities: Implement data access using multiple ADO.NET patterns within a cross-functional team.
impact: Supported consolidated monitoring of schools, teachers, children, and annual education reports.

records.title: Enterprise Archive
records.summary: A guided view of a documented imaging flow, from protocol boundary through durable archive services to downstream availability.

nodes.label: Imaging producer
nodes.purpose: Create or submit an imaging study.

nodes.label: Protocol router
nodes.purpose: Validate and route imaging data into archive workflows.
nodes.whySelected: Protects the core workflow from protocol variance.

nodes.label: Archive services
nodes.purpose: Coordinate durable storage, retrieval, and availability.
nodes.whySelected: Centralizes the domain responsibility without exposing implementation details.

nodes.label: Clinical consumer
nodes.purpose: Make a study available to a consuming workflow.

records.title: Distributed Messaging
records.summary: A concise reference flow showing how explicit messages, retries, and terminal-failure paths keep services decoupled.

nodes.label: API boundary
nodes.purpose: Accept a command and acknowledge intent.
nodes.whySelected: Separates request handling from long-running work.

nodes.label: Message broker
nodes.whySelected: Provides a clear buffer for asynchronous workloads.

nodes.label: Workflow worker
nodes.purpose: Perform the domain operation and report outcome.

nodes.label: Terminal-failure queue
nodes.purpose: Hold work that needs investigation or replay.
nodes.whySelected: Makes failure a designed path rather than a hidden timeout.

records.title: Use service and API boundaries
records.problem: Capabilities needed to evolve without coupling every workflow change.
records.decision: Use explicit service and API boundaries where the documented workflow benefits from independent evolution.
records.outcome: A clearer map from business responsibility to engineering ownership.
records.lesson: Boundaries are useful when they make change safer, not when they add ceremony.

records.title: Treat automated tests as a release-quality control
records.problem: Regression risk rises as enterprise workflows and integrations grow.
options: Automated backend coverage plus targeted regression checks
records.decision: Use automated tests as a repeatable quality gate.
records.outcome: More confidence when changing integration-heavy workflows.
records.lesson: Quality is a system property created by habits, not a final inspection step.

records.title: Label architecture evidence clearly
records.problem: Public architecture stories can sound more certain than their evidence.
constraints: Separate documented facts from reference patterns
options: Use evidence states and text equivalents
records.decision: Use documented/reference labels and concise text equivalents.
records.outcome: Readers can explore the reasoning through a concise engineering blueprint.
records.lesson: Trust grows when the engineering context is clear.

records.title: Imaging exchange: architecture notes
records.overview: A short preview of ingestion, archive, and retrieval responsibilities in an imaging workflow.
decisions: Keep domain-specific handling at the imaging boundary
Make availability and failure handling explicit

records.title: Event-driven workflows: architecture notes
records.overview: A concise look at why asynchronous communication can help isolate workflow responsibilities.
decisions: Keep retry and terminal-failure paths visible

records.title: Enterprise automation: architecture notes
records.overview: A high-level view of adapter boundaries across heterogeneous enterprise systems.
decisions: Keep orchestration separate from target-system details
